## Houston 0.4.15

Patch on top of 0.4.14. Repairs the **Continue with Google** flow on Windows that left users stuck on the sign-in screen even though Supabase had already accepted the tokens.

### Fixed

- **Continue with Google on Windows now lands you on the dashboard on the first click.** On 0.4.14 the OAuth callback was being received correctly and Supabase was installing the session in memory, but the auth gate in the app was reading from a TanStack Query cache that depended on Supabase's `onAuthStateChange` listener firing. On real Windows MSI installs that listener was not propagating the `SIGNED_IN` event for the implicit-flow code path. The frontend log showed `[auth] session established (implicit) for <email>` line after line, but the UI never transitioned. The deep-link handler now writes the new session directly into the cache key the gate reads from, so the transition happens deterministically regardless of whether the listener fires. New `[auth] onAuthStateChange fired: <event>` log line on every event lets us tell from a bug report whether the listener is the actual problem or whether the direct cache write is the only thing keeping the UI in sync.

### Before you upgrade

- **Mac:** quit Houston before installing. macOS does not always replace a running app cleanly. If in doubt, remove `/Applications/Houston.app` and drag the new copy in.
- **Windows x64:** auto-update from 0.4.7 or later pulls you forward automatically. The MSI is still not OS-code-signed (SignPath integration is still pending), so expect one SmartScreen "Run anyway" prompt on first install.
- **Windows ARM64:** there is no auto-update path from an emulated x64 install. Download the ARM64 MSI manually, uninstall the x64 build first, then install the ARM64 one. Future ARM64 to ARM64 updates apply automatically.

### If you were stuck on the 0.4.14 sign-in screen on Windows

Auto-update to 0.4.15 from 0.4.14 should pull you forward automatically if you can get Houston open long enough for the check to run. If you cannot, download the 0.4.15 MSI from the release page and install over the top. Your local data is preserved.

### Known limitations

- **Windows MSI not yet OS-code-signed.** SignPath integration still pending. One SmartScreen "Run anyway" click on first install only, both x64 and ARM64.
- **"Continue with Microsoft" sign-in for Houston itself remains hidden** while we reconcile the Supabase Azure provider with the production callback URL. Google sign-in is unaffected. The Microsoft 365 + Outlook tutorial path is unaffected.
- **Mobile companion is still beta.** Expect occasional reconnect hiccups. Keep your Mac awake while using it.
